criteria.label: Define data augmentation and explain its role as overfitting mitigation.
criteria.example: Write one sentence: label-preserving transforms expand training diversity to reduce memorization when val error exceeds train error.

criteria.label: List appropriate image, text, and tabular augmentation techniques.
criteria.example: Image: flip + crop; text: synonym swap or back-translation; tabular: Gaussian noise; mention SMOTE for minority imbalance without deriving formulas.

criteria.label: Design train vs validation preprocessing for an image classifier.
criteria.example: Propose train RandomResizedCrop+Flip and val Resize+CenterCrop only — same 224×224 output, no random val transforms.

criteria.label: Identify when a transform violates label semantics.
criteria.example: Explain why 90° rotation on upright MNIST digits can swap 6/9 labels and recommend ±15° max or no rotation.

criteria.label: Explain correct SMOTE usage in an imbalanced tabular workflow.
criteria.example: Outline: stratified split → SMOTE.fit_resample on X_train only → train classifier → evaluate on untouched X_test.
